message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Fix: ExplorePage content overflowing center-bar-holder-container

## Context
The explore page content overflows/clips because the `<motion.div>` wrapper around `<Outlet>` in Home.jsx has no width or height — it's an unsized intermediate div that breaks the layout chain. The `overflow-y: auto` on `.center-bar-holder-container` (already added) isn't enough because the motion.div doesn't fill its parent.

## Root Cause
In `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (lines 328-338), the `<AnimatePresence>` + `<motion.div>` wrapping the `<Outlet>` has no width, height, or flex properties. This unsized div prevents child content from properly filling `.center-bar-holder-container`.

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (line 329)
Add `style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` to the `<motion.div>`:

```jsx
<motion.div
    key={location.pathname}
    initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}
    animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
    transition={{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0.25, 1, 0.5, 1] }}
    style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}
>
```

This ensures the motion.div fills `.center-bar-holder-container` so child pages (ExplorePage, PostCards, ContentView, etc.) can properly size and scroll within it.

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css`
Already done (previous edit): `overflow-y: auto` added to `.center-bar-holder-container` at all 4 breakpoints.

## Verification
- `/home/explore` — hero card, runners, interest sections all visible and scrollable
- `/home` (feed) — feed still scrolls correctly
- `/home/post/:id` — content view still works
- Resize to mobile — same behavior, no clipping


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\d45a28f9-2c3c-4f2a-afd7-74d667e908ec.jsonl

## Context
The explore page content overflows/clips because the `<motion.div>` wrapper around `<Outlet>` in Home.jsx has no width or height — it's an unsized intermediate div that breaks the layout chain. The `overflow-y: auto` on `.center-bar-holder-container` (already added) isn't enough because the motion.div doesn't fill its parent.

## Root Cause
In `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (lines 328-338), the `<AnimatePresence>` + `<motion.div>` wrapping the `<Outlet>` has no width, height, or flex properties. This unsized div prevents child content from properly filling `.center-bar-holder-container`.

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (line 329)
Add `style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` to the `<motion.div>`:

```jsx
<motion.div
    key={location.pathname}
    initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}
    animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
    transition={{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0.25, 1, 0.5, 1] }}
    style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}
>
```

This ensures the motion.div fills `.center-bar-holder-container` so child pages (ExplorePage, PostCards, ContentView, etc.) can properly size and scroll within it.

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css`
Already done (previous edit): `overflow-y: auto` added to `.center-bar-holder-container` at all 4 breakpoints.

## Verification
- `/home/explore` — hero card, runners, interest sections all visible and scrollable
- `/home` (feed) — feed still scrolls correctly
- `/home/post/:id` — content view still works
- Resize to mobile — same behavior, no clipping

file.content:
# Fix: ExplorePage content overflowing center-bar-holder-container

## Context
The explore page content overflows/clips because the `<motion.div>` wrapper around `<Outlet>` in Home.jsx has no width or height — it's an unsized intermediate div that breaks the layout chain. The `overflow-y: auto` on `.center-bar-holder-container` (already added) isn't enough because the motion.div doesn't fill its parent.

## Root Cause
In `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (lines 328-338), the `<AnimatePresence>` + `<motion.div>` wrapping the `<Outlet>` has no width, height, or flex properties. This unsized div prevents child content from properly filling `.center-bar-holder-container`.

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (line 329)
Add `style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` to the `<motion.div>`:

```jsx
<motion.div
    key={location.pathname}
    initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}
    animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
    transition={{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0.25, 1, 0.5, 1] }}
    style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}
>
```

This ensures the motion.div fills `.center-bar-holder-container` so child pages (ExplorePage, PostCards, ContentView, etc.) can properly size and scroll within it.

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css`
Already done (previous edit): `overflow-y: auto` added to `.center-bar-holder-container` at all 4 breakpoints.

## Verification
- `/home/explore` — hero card, runners, interest sections all visible and scrollable
- `/home` (feed) — feed still scrolls correctly
- `/home/post/:id` — content view still works
- Resize to mobile — same behavior, no clipping

content.text:
Perfect! I now have all the information I need. Let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis of how `.ov-reply-input` is used in nested replies.

## Summary of `.ov-reply-input` Usage in Nested Replies

### 1. **JSX Files Using `.ov-reply-input`**

Two files use the `.ov-reply-input` class:

- **`OpinionsCardRepy.jsx`** (line 150) — Used for nested reply inputs in the reply thread
- **`opinionViewer.jsx`** (line 122) — Used for top-level reply input in the opinion detail view

### 2. **CSS Rules for `.ov-reply-input`**

Located at `/client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/sidebarOpinions.css` (lines 973-993):

```css
.ov-reply-input {
    flex: 1;
    min-height: 1.4rem;
    outline: none;
    border: none;
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
    background-color: transparent;
    font-family: var(--font-ui);
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    font-style: normal;
    color: var(--text-primary);
    padding-bottom: 0.25rem;
    resize: none;
    transition: border-color 0.2s ease;
}
```

**Key property causing width issue:** `flex: 1` — This makes the input expand to fill available space.

### 3. **Nested Reply Structure & Layout Chain**

The nested reply structure in `OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` follows this hierarchy:

```
.rc-thread (line 1052)
  └─ .rc-reply (line 1058)
      ├─ .rc-avatar-col (line 1064)
      │   ├─ .so-avatar-outer
      │   └─ .rc-connector (vertical line)
      └─ .rc-content (line 1086)
          ├─ .so-header-row
          ├─ .rc-body
          ├─ .rc-actions
          └─ .rc-inline-composer (line 1167)
              └─ .ov-reply-input
```

### 4. **Width Constraint Issue for Nested Replies**

**The Problem:**
- `.rc-thread` has `padding-left: calc(var(--depth, 0) * 1rem)` (line 1053) — adds left padding for indentation
- `.rc-reply` is a flex container (line 1058-1062)
- `.rc-content` has `flex: 1; min-width: 0;` (lines 1087-1091)
- `.rc-inline-composer` inherits the flex layout (lines 1167-1173)
- `.ov-reply-input` has `flex: 1` (line 974) inside a flex column

**Why nested replies are too wide:**
The `.ov-reply-input` with `flex: 1` expands to fill the width of `.rc-content`, but `.rc-content` itself is constrained by the `.rc-reply` flex layout. At deeper nesting levels:
- The `.rc-thread` padding compounds: `depth * 1rem`
- But the input doesn't account for the cumulative left padding from ancestor threads
- The parent container (likely the `latest-opinions-container` at line 53-65) has `width: 90%`, but nested `.rc-thread` elements keep expanding rightward

### 5. **Key Container Widths**

- **`.latest-opinions-container`** (line 53-65): `width: 90%` with `overflow-x: hidden`
- **`.rc-thread`** (line 1052-1056): `width: 100%; padding-left: calc(var(--depth, 0) * 1rem)`
- **`.ov-composer-input-wrap`** in `opinionViewer.jsx` (line 954-959): Has `flex: 1; min-width: 0` which constrains the input properly

The top-level reply input in `opinionViewer.jsx` works correctly because it's wrapped in `.ov-composer-input-wrap` (a flex container with proper constraints), but nested replies in `OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` only have `.rc-inline-composer` which doesn't have the same width constraints.

### **Root Cause**
The `.ov-reply-input` in nested replies (`OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` line 150) is wider than intended because:
1. It's inside `.rc-inline-composer` (flex column) which doesn't have `min-width: 0`
2. The cumulative indentation from `.rc-thread` padding increases at each depth level
3. The textarea tries to expand to `flex: 1` without horizontal constraints from parent containers

---

## File Locations
- **JSX component with nested replies:** `/C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\SidebarOpinions\OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` (lines 140-173)
- **CSS styling:** `/C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\SidebarOpinions\sidebarOpinions.css` (lines 973-993, 1052-1192)
- **Top-level reply viewer (for comparison):** `/C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\SidebarOpinions\opinionViewer.jsx` (lines 107-159)

- **JSX component with nested replies:** `/C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\SidebarOpinions\OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` (lines 140-173)

```jsx
<motion.div
    key={location.pathname}
    initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}
    animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
    transition={{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0.25, 1, 0.5, 1] }}
```

input.new_string:
# Fix: `.ov-reply-input` too wide in nested replies

## Context
In the SidebarOpinions panel, when replying to a nested reply, the `.ov-reply-input` textarea stretches too wide, overflowing its container.

## Root Cause
The layout chain for nested reply inputs is:

```
.rc-thread  (padding-left: depth * 1rem)
  └─ .rc-reply  (flex row)
      └─ .rc-content  (flex: 1, min-width: 0) ✓ constrained
          └─ .rc-inline-composer  (flex column, overflow: hidden — but NO min-width: 0)
              └─ .ov-reply-input  (flex: 1)
```

`.rc-inline-composer` (line 1167) is a flex child of `.rc-content` but lacks `min-width: 0`. In flexbox, children default to `min-width: auto`, which prevents them from shrinking below their content size. The textarea inside expands beyond the available width at deeper nesting levels.

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/sidebarOpinions.css` (line 1167)
Add `min-width: 0` to `.rc-inline-composer`:

```css
.rc-inline-composer {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    overflow: hidden;
    padding-top: 0.25rem;
    min-width: 0;
}
```

This lets `.rc-inline-composer` shrink properly within the flex chain, so `.ov-reply-input` stays within bounds at any nesting depth.

## Verification
- Open an opinion with nested replies, click "Reply" on a deeply nested reply — input should not overflow
- Top-level reply input in opinionViewer should remain unchanged
- Resize sidebar / mobile — no horizontal overflow

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (line 329)
Add `style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` to the `<motion.div>`:

```jsx
<motion.div
    key={location.pathname}
    initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}
    animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
    transition={{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0.25, 1, 0.5, 1] }}
    style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}
>
```

This ensures the motion.div fills `.center-bar-holder-container` so child pages (ExplorePage, PostCards, ContentView, etc.) can properly size and scroll within it.

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css`
Already done (previous edit): `overflow-y: auto` added to `.center-bar-holder-container` at all 4 breakpoints.

## Verification
- `/home/explore` — hero card, runners, interest sections all visible and scrollable
- `/home` (feed) — feed still scrolls correctly
- `/home/post/:id` — content view still works
- Resize to mobile — same behavior, no clipping
toolUseResult.newString:
# Fix: `.ov-reply-input` too wide in nested replies

## Context
In the SidebarOpinions panel, when replying to a nested reply, the `.ov-reply-input` textarea stretches too wide, overflowing its container.

## Root Cause
The layout chain for nested reply inputs is:

```
.rc-thread  (padding-left: depth * 1rem)
  └─ .rc-reply  (flex row)
      └─ .rc-content  (flex: 1, min-width: 0) ✓ constrained
          └─ .rc-inline-composer  (flex column, overflow: hidden — but NO min-width: 0)
              └─ .ov-reply-input  (flex: 1)
```

`.rc-inline-composer` (line 1167) is a flex child of `.rc-content` but lacks `min-width: 0`. In flexbox, children default to `min-width: auto`, which prevents them from shrinking below their content size. The textarea inside expands beyond the available width at deeper nesting levels.

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/sidebarOpinions.css` (line 1167)
Add `min-width: 0` to `.rc-inline-composer`:

```css
.rc-inline-composer {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    overflow: hidden;
    padding-top: 0.25rem;
    min-width: 0;
}
```

This lets `.rc-inline-composer` shrink properly within the flex chain, so `.ov-reply-input` stays within bounds at any nesting depth.

## Verification
- Open an opinion with nested replies, click "Reply" on a deeply nested reply — input should not overflow
- Top-level reply input in opinionViewer should remain unchanged
- Resize sidebar / mobile — no horizontal overflow
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Fix: ExplorePage content overflowing center-bar-holder-container

## Context
The explore page content overflows/clips because the `<motion.div>` wrapper around `<Outlet>` in Home.jsx has no width or height — it's an unsized intermediate div that breaks the layout chain. The `overflow-y: auto` on `.center-bar-holder-container` (already added) isn't enough because the motion.div doesn't fill its parent.

## Root Cause
In `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (lines 328-338), the `<AnimatePresence>` + `<motion.div>` wrapping the `<Outlet>` has no width, height, or flex properties. This unsized div prevents child content from properly filling `.center-bar-holder-container`.

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` (line 329)
Add `style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` to the `<motion.div>`:

```jsx
<motion.div
    key={location.pathname}
    initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 8 }}
    animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
    exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
    transition={{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0.25, 1, 0.5, 1] }}
    style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}
>
```

This ensures the motion.div fills `.center-bar-holder-container` so child pages (ExplorePage, PostCards, ContentView, etc.) can properly size and scroll within it.

### File: `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css`
Already done (previous edit): `overflow-y: auto` added to `.center-bar-holder-container` at all 4 breakpoints.

## Verification
- `/home/explore` — hero card, runners, interest sections all visible and scrollable
- `/home` (feed) — feed still scrolls correctly
- `/home/post/:id` — content view still works
- Resize to mobile — same behavior, no clipping

# Fix: `.ov-reply-input` too wide in nested replies

## Context
In the SidebarOpinions panel, when replying to a nested reply, the `.ov-reply-input` textarea stretches too wide, overflowing its container.

## Root Cause
The layout chain for nested reply inputs is:

```
.rc-thread  (padding-left: depth * 1rem)
  └─ .rc-reply  (flex row)
      └─ .rc-content  (flex: 1, min-width: 0) ✓ constrained
          └─ .rc-inline-composer  (flex column, overflow: hidden — but NO min-width: 0)
              └─ .ov-reply-input  (flex: 1)
```

`.rc-inline-composer` (line 1167) is a flex child of `.rc-content` but lacks `min-width: 0`. In flexbox, children default to `min-width: auto`, which prevents them from shrinking below their content size. The textarea inside expands beyond the available width at deeper nesting levels.

## Fix

### File: `client/src/components/SidebarOpinions/sidebarOpinions.css` (line 1167)
Add `min-width: 0` to `.rc-inline-composer`:

```css
.rc-inline-composer {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    overflow: hidden;
    padding-top: 0.25rem;
    min-width: 0;
}
```

This lets `.rc-inline-composer` shrink properly within the flex chain, so `.ov-reply-input` stays within bounds at any nesting depth.

## Verification
- Open an opinion with nested replies, click "Reply" on a deeply nested reply — input should not overflow
- Top-level reply input in opinionViewer should remain unchanged
- Resize sidebar / mobile — no horizontal overflow
